response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior full-stack engineer with genuine systems-level work (low-latency DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second) and a strong mentoring track record, but he clearly misses the core minimum qualification of 8 years of C++ — his C/C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, and C++ is absent from his skills list. His infrastructure experience is real but fragmented and product-oriented rather than sustained large-scale platform work, and he has no formal technical-leadership role leading cloud or DevOps engineers, which this role centers on.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing record (workshops, multi-day courses, conference talks, open-source test tooling)
Broad 15+ year career across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded with solid engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ minimum — only a brief C/C++ stint, and C++ is not listed in his skills
Large-scale infrastructure and cloud platform experience is intermittent (~2 years deep systems work) versus the 5-year requirement
No formal technical-leadership role owning cross-team strategy or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers

key_factors.factor: Limited sustained large-scale infrastructure/cloud platform work
